review_comment:
Since we are also doing `set_data->len - 2` maybe we should store the result in a variable here which would provide the semantic meaning of why we subtract 2, e.g. something like
```
uint8_t data_len = set_data->len - 2; /* Length of the data minus the length and type fields */
```

I also see that we actually even have the `len` variable which is what actually encode into the length field, so perhaps this should actually have been
```suggestion
					memcpy(&set_data->data[2], data[j].data, len);
```

Any changes to this should probably also be used in the offset calculation on the next line.

The `len` variable is the length of the AD field. This can be larger than `BT_HCI_LE_EXT_ADV_FRAG_MAX_LEN`, therefore we cannot apply the patch you've suggested. 

We could consider extracting out `uint8_t used_ad_len` or something similar.

In any case, we should probably also add (void) to the call to `memcpy`:
```suggestion
					(void)memcpy(&set_data->data[2], data[j].data, set_data->len - 2);
```
